ip_outcome_note: Named legendary creature — strongest possible IP confirmation. Scryfall type_line: 'Legendary Creature — Human Cleric'; card name IS the character name. The depicted figure is definitionally the named legendary. Whisper is a Wizards-internal character; no external IP callout block required. Whisper character node does not yet exist in cards/_characters/; flagged for future commissioning (she has enough canonical lore depth from MTG Wiki and EDHREC article). Frontmatter updated: ip_verified: false -> true.

trivia_bullets.text: Whisper is a human Cabal cleric on Dominaria, a street-orphan of Estark who joined the Cabal as her first opportunity for power. She cultivated the habit of speaking quietly so others had to lean toward her — the source of her name. By the Dominaria storyline she had risen to become one of Belzenlok's favored priests, leading ceremonies at the Cabal Stronghold.
sources: MTG Wiki — Whisper, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: 'Look how they beg for the knife! The Demonlord has trained them well.' The 'Demonlord' is Belzenlok. The quote positions Whisper as a cold observer of her congregation's willing submission — supplicants trained into accepting sacrifice as devotion.

Whisper's ability — {T}, Sacrifice two creatures: Return target creature card from your graveyard to the battlefield — requires no mana, making her a zero-cost reanimation engine in creature-heavy black decks. Key ruling: neither sacrificed creature can be the reanimation target, closing potential infinite-ETB loops. EDHREC rank 5759.
sources: EDHREC — Underdog's Corner: Whisper, Blood Liturgist

trivia_bullets.text: The Cabal on Dominaria was reconstituted under Belzenlok sixty years after the Mending, relocating from Otaria to Urborg. Whisper's flavor text — supplicants trained to desire the sacrifice blade — is consistent with Cabal doctrine treating life as transactional currency.
sources: Wizards of the Coast — Return to Dominaria story series; WebSearch snippet MTG Wiki — Cabal

trivia_bullets.text: Illustrated by Kieran Yanner, who also illustrated Liliana's Triumph (WAR-98), the black member of the Gatewatch Triumph cycle — making Yanner a recurring artist for black-aligned Wizards-narrative cards.
